purpose.summary: The COBOL program PAUDBUNL extracts pending authorization summary and detail records from an IMS database and writes them to sequential output files. It reads authorization summary records and then, for each summary record, reads associated detail records, writing each to separate output files.

inputs.name: PAUTBPCB
inputs.description: IMS PCB used to access the authorization summary and detail segments.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: Authorization summary segment read from the IMS database.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: Authorization detail segment read from the IMS database.

outputs.name: OPFILE1
outputs.description: Sequential file containing authorization summary records.

outputs.name: OPFILE2
outputs.description: Sequential file containing authorization detail records.

called_programs.purpose: Issue DL/I calls to the IMS database.
Issue DL/I calls to the IMS database.

transforms.transformation_description: Moves the account ID from the pending authorization summary record to the root segment key.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the program identifier paragraph. It does not contain any executable code and serves only to name the program.

This paragraph serves as the main control loop for the program. It first calls 1000-INITIALIZE to perform necessary setup tasks such as opening files. Then, it repeatedly calls 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY to read and process authorization summary records until the end of the IMS database is reached, indicated by WS-END-OF-ROOT-SEG being set to 'Y'. Finally, it calls 4000-FILE-CLOSE to close the output files before terminating the program with a GOBACK statement.

This paragraph initializes the program by accepting the current date and displaying startup messages. It opens OPFILE1 and OPFILE2 for output. If either OPEN fails (WS-OUTFL1-STATUS or WS-OUTFL2-STATUS not equal to spaces or '00'), it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The paragraph ensures that the output files are ready for writing before the main processing loop begins. The current date and day are accepted from the system and displayed for logging purposes.
This paragraph is a simple exit point for the 1000-INITIALIZE paragraph, allowing for a structured PERFORM THRU.

This paragraph retrieves the next authorization summary record from the IMS database. It initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS and then calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-GN to retrieve the next root segment (authorization summary). If the call is successful (PAUT-PCB-STATUS = SPACES), it increments counters, moves the retrieved data to OPFIL1-REC, initializes ROOT-SEG-KEY and CHILD-SEG-REC, moves PA-ACCT-ID to ROOT-SEG-KEY, and writes the OPFIL1-REC to OPFILE1 if PA-ACCT-ID is numeric. Then, it calls 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL to retrieve the associated detail records. If the PCB status is 'GB', it sets END-OF-AUTHDB to TRUE and WS-END-OF-ROOT-SEG to 'Y', indicating the end of the database. If the PCB status is neither spaces nor 'GB', it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND.
This paragraph is a simple exit point for the 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph, allowing for a structured PERFORM THRU.

This paragraph retrieves the next authorization detail record from the IMS database for the current authorization summary record. It calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-GNP to retrieve the next child segment (authorization detail). If the call is successful (PAUT-PCB-STATUS = SPACES), it sets MORE-AUTHS to TRUE, increments counters, moves the retrieved data to CHILD-SEG-REC, and writes the OPFIL2-REC to OPFILE2. If the PCB status is 'GE', it moves 'Y' to WS-END-OF-CHILD-SEG, indicating no more detail records exist for the current summary record. If the PCB status is neither spaces nor 'GE', it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND. Finally, it initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS.
This paragraph is a simple exit point for the 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL paragraph, allowing for a structured PERFORM THRU.

This paragraph closes the output files OPFILE1 and OPFILE2. It displays a message indicating that the files are being closed. After each CLOSE statement, it checks the file status (WS-OUTFL1-STATUS and WS-OUTFL2-STATUS). If the status is not spaces or '00', it displays an error message, but does NOT abend.
This paragraph is a simple exit point for the 4000-FILE-CLOSE paragraph, allowing for a structured PERFORM THRU.

error_handling.condition: WS-OUTFL1-STATUS NOT = SPACES OR '00' (OPFILE1 OPEN error)
WS-OUTFL2-STATUS NOT = SPACES OR '00' (OPFILE2 OPEN error)
PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'GB' (AUTH SUM  GN failed)
PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'GE' (GNP CALL FAILED)

open_questions.context: The copybooks for these segments are not provided.
